name: Dirac leptogenesis
summary: Generates the baryon asymmetry without invoking Majorana neutrino masses. CP-violating decays of heavy particles produce equal and opposite asymmetries in the SM lepton doublets and the right-handed neutrinos, but the Dirac Yukawa coupling is small enough that the right-handed-neutrino asymmetry stays out of equilibrium with the SM. The SM-side lepton asymmetry is then converted to baryon asymmetry by sphalerons. Demonstrates that lepton-number conservation is compatible with leptogenesis-style BAU.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces eta ~ 10^-10 for heavy Yukawa-decaying particles in the 10^9-10^15 GeV range. Predicts Dirac (not Majorana) neutrinos — falsifiable by 0nubb non-detection at sensitivities reaching the inverted-hierarchy floor.

Right-handed neutrinos couple via small Yukawas (typical ~10^-12); the small Dirac Yukawa is tied to the asymmetry's not-yet-equilibrating, providing a connection between BAU and the smallness of m_nu.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicts NULL result for 0nubb at all sensitivities — neutrinos are Dirac, not Majorana. Future experiments KamLAND-Zen, LEGEND, nEXO can falsify by detecting 0nubb.

new_fields: heavy decaying particles producing the asymmetry
introduces.note: Heavy-particle masses, decay rates, CP phases, Dirac Yukawa structure.

history: Dick, Lindner, Ratz, Wright (1999) introduced Dirac leptogenesis. Murayama and Pierce (2002), 'Realistic Dirac Leptogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 89, 271601, arXiv:hep-ph/0206177 — concrete realistic model. Active small-community follow-up; experimentally distinguished from Majorana leptogenesis by 0nubb null result.

references.citation: Murayama and Pierce, 'Realistic Dirac leptogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 89, 271601 (2002)
